page: Microsoft Azure F2ams_v6 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Famsv6 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. The server features 2 dedicated physical cores with 2 vCPUs, meaning there is no hyperthreading, resulting in 1.0 thread per core. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, alongside support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16) and cryptography standards like AES and SHA_NI. It does not include local storage or GPUs, but is premium storage capable.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling. In stress-ng single-core tests, the server ranks in the top 10% with a score of 3080.08, and its Geekbench single-core score of 2066.0 is in the top 25%. However, multi-core benchmarks show a drop in relative standing due to the low core count; the Geekbench multi-core score of 3838.0 and the stress-ng multi-core score of 6118.06 fall into the bottom 25% and middle 50% respectively. Memory bandwidth for large blocks is average at 38.9 GB/sec read and 28.08 GB/sec write, while small-block cached read bandwidth is weak at 294.38 GB/sec.

The F2ams_v6 offers a memory-intensive profile with a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that require substantial RAM relative to compute power without needing high core counts. However, the lack of local storage and the low multi-core performance present clear tradeoffs for parallel processing tasks, as seen in the weak Passmark database operations and Redis SET benchmarks. This server is suited for single-threaded applications, light memory-intensive tasks, text processing, and small-scale LLM inference using small models, where single-core speed and memory capacity are prioritized over multi-threaded throughput.
description: Microsoft Azure F2ams_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine in the Famsv6 family, powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores (2 vCPUs) and 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The server lacks local storage and GPUs but supports premium storage. Performance benchmarks highlight strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance ranking in the top 10% and Geekbench single-core in the top 25%. Conversely, multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks generally fall into the average to weak categories due to the low core count. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive, low-concurrency tasks. It is best suited for single-threaded applications, text processing, and small-scale language model inference where memory capacity is required but parallel compute demands are minimal.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F2ams_v6 is a memory-intensive x86_64 virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 16 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance, suited for low-concurrency workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F2ams_v6 is an x86_64 VM with 2 AMD EPYC cores, 16 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-intensive dual-core virtual machine delivering strong single-threaded performance and a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for specialized workloads.
bullet_points: Dual-core AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz
16.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
Top-tier single-core performance in stress-ng benchmarks
No local storage, with support for premium storage options
Suited for single-threaded applications and small-scale LLM inference
